text:
Market today: Mixed-to-cautious mood. The real driver is macro noise around data centers and AI infrastructure hitting sentiment exactly where your book lives — chip/tech names are under pressure even as geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz pause) gives a small relief pop to futures.

Your holdings: AVGO is the one to stare at — your largest position, down 16% and flagged SELL. The "data center suddenly suspect" narrative is a direct headwind; if it breaks toward that $336 stop on margin, the damage accelerates fast. PLTR is down 13% and also SELL — no specific catalyst today but the broad tech skepticism doesn't help. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — thin liquidity means wild swings; margin makes that extra dangerous.

Watch: (1) Any follow-through on the data center/AI spending doubt story — that's AVGO and CRWD's neighborhood. (2) Whether the Iran/Hormuz pause holds; oil stability matters for broad market mood.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are screaming for a hard decision — trim or cut AVGO and PLTR before margin turns a bad trade into a disaster.
